title: About
text: My name is Noora Mohamed, I am a junior at the University of Nebraska, Lincoln. I double major in Sports media and Journalism, with a minor in Communication studies and Textile, Merchandising fashion design. I recently started my masters in Integrated media Communication at UNL.

title: HOW DOES CHANGING IN COACHES AFFECT A RANKING OF A TEAM
text:
The results of a game, is a reflection of how a coaching is doing behind closed doors. Coaches are expected to train, discipline and prepare their athletes for every possibility in the field.
Fans focus on the winning and scoring, the real impact all comes back through the planning and knowledge, that coaches implant in their players.
The score of a single game, leads back to the training that happens before a game, and how much effort is put into the players, revealing the ability the coach has when it comes to a team performance.
Álvaro Arbeloa, a professional Spanish former defender football player and current manager of Real Madrid. Arbeloa has won many trophies; Club World Cup, a UEFA Super Cup, a LaLiga Title, two Copas del Rey and a Spanish Super Cup. His ability to train young athelets, has proven what a person really learns and teaches others has an imapct.
Xabi Alonso a former Midfielder football player and a former coach in Real Madrid. Alonso has played in about 236 competitive games, wining over 9 trophies during his time as a football player, his trophies included one LaLiga title and two European Championships with Spain. Alonso is considered as one of the best midfilers of his generations.
Carlo Ancellotti, the former coach of Real Madrid, is one of the ost decorated coach in the history of Real Madrid, wining over 15 trophies. Ancellotti has started his career in July 2009, coaching AC Milan for eight years. Before transitioning into
How much does a change of coach play in team ranking?


Code
library(tidyverse)
madrid <- read_csv("LALIGA25-26.csv",show_col_types = FALSE) |> filter(Pts >0)|> mutate(season="25-26")
madrid1 <- read_csv("LALIGA24-25.csv",show_col_types = FALSE)|> filter(Pts>0)|>
mutate(season="24-25")
madrid2<- read_csv("LALIGA23-24.csv",show_col_types = FALSE)|> filter(Pts>0)|>
mutate(season="23-24")
madrid_all<-bind_rows(madrid,madrid1,madrid2)
ggplot(madrid_all,aes(x=reorder(Name, RK), weight=Pts, fill=season))+
 geom_bar(position="dodge")+
             coord_flip()+
      labs(title = "Athelete performance during the LaLiga season 23-26",
       subtitle = "Bar Charts,higlighting the difference player ranking acroos all three three seasons",
       x = "Total points ",
       y = "Players Name",
       caption = "Source: ESPN|By Noora Mohamed")+
  theme_minimal()+
theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 15, face = "plain")) 









For my hypothesis, I used ESPN to analyze and understand how coaching change, has an impact on the team’s skills and ranking. After, studying the bar charts, I discovered that a team player’s performance and ranking, demonstrates the skills and proficiency of the coach.
For instance, Dean Huijsen is a Defender for Real Madrid during the LaLiga season he was number one ranked, when he was coached by Alvaro Arbeloa, who was also a former defender player. The bar chart results, demonstrates that when a coach changes it can in fact have an impact on team performances, which is very to important to understand that when coaches apply their experience on young athletes the field, the results speak louder.


Code
library(tidyverse)
madrid25<- read_csv("25RFBF.csv") 
madrid24<- read_csv("24FBREF.csv") 
madrid23<- read_csv("23-24fb.csv") 
ggplot() + 
  geom_point(data=madrid25, aes(x=GF, y=GA,), color="red") + 
  geom_point(data=madrid24, aes(x=GF, y=GA), color="hotpink") +
  geom_point(data=madrid23, aes(x=GF, y=GA), color="blue") + 
  geom_vline(xintercept = 5.863151) + 
  geom_hline(yintercept = 5.448228) +
  geom_text(aes(x=15, y=40, label="2025-2026"), color="red") +
  geom_text(aes(x=52, y=26, label="2024-2025"), color="hotpink") +
  geom_text(aes(x=30, y=76, label="2023-2024"), color="blue")+
  labs(title = "Real Madrid Goals scored vs. Goals scored again in LaLiga (2023-2026) ",
       subtitle = "A graph that shows how well Readl Madrid did during the season with offensive and defensive play",
       x = "GOALS FOR",
       y = "GAMES AGAINST",
       caption = "Source: FootballRef|By: Noora Mohamed") 









When trying to understand the various ways Real Madrid played during the LaLiga season, it’s important to look at goals such as; Goals For (GF) and Goals against (GA) utilizing these two different stats. Gives us a chance to understand the strength the team has when tackling with goals.
For instance, during the 2024-2025 season, Real Madrid has scored about 53 goals with the highest goals against opponent being 80 proving, that during that season Real Madrid had a strong offense, rather than defense. Studying a chart about goals isn’t only about the goals that they were able to get, but rather a result of how much effort and training was put in the offensive side.


Code
library(tidyverse)
library(patchwork) 
gamess <- read_csv("25_26.csv",show_col_types = FALSE) 
gamesss<- read_csv("23_24.csv",show_col_types = FALSE) 
bar1 <- ggplot() + 
  geom_col(data=gamess, aes(x=reorder(Pts, Position),y=LALIGA), fill="blue") + geom_col(data=gamesss, aes(x=reorder(Pts, Position),y=LALIGA), fill="red") + coord_flip() + 
  labs( x="Points", y="Team Ranking") + 
  theme_minimal() 
bar2 <- 
  ggplot() + geom_col(data=gamesss, aes(x=reorder(Pts, Wins),y=LALIGA), fill="darkgrey") + 
  geom_col(data=gamess, aes(x=reorder(Pts, Wins),y=LALIGA), fill="pink") + coord_flip() +
  labs( x="Points", y="Team") + 
  theme_minimal() 
bar1/bar2 + 
  plot_annotation( title = "Real Madrid LALIGA ranking", 
  subtitle = "During the LALIGA 2023 season Real Madrid was ranked number one by 95 points. Following the 2026 season, Real Madrid is now ranked second, with Barcelona taking the lead by 76 points.",
  caption = "Source: Yahoo Sports | By Noora Mohamed" ) & theme ( 
  plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"), 
  axis.title = element_text(size = 8), plot.subtitle = element_text(size=10), panel.grid.minor = element_blank() )









To better understand the difference of ranking, I ranked the team as a whole to see how the ranking has changed and what actually happened that led to these results. I compared 2023-2024 and 2025-2026, which gave a result of how the performance of the team has changed.
For instance, during the 23-24 season Real Madrid scored 95 points making them first ranked, with wins about 85 points, making Barcelona second ranked at the time.
Looking at the ranking of a team, tells a story, not only about the coaching, but rather the results that speak louder than words. Looking at these charts have proven that changes in coaches, will have an impact on the team performance. It is clear that a coach can be good at training an offensive side than coaching a defensive side.
